originalTitle: Oregon’s Attorney General withdraws effort to delay Paramount and Warner Bros. merger
summary: Oregon Attorney General Dan Rayfield had been seeking documents from Paramount related to its takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery. Rayfield also asked a state circuit court judge to delay the closing of the deal by 60 days so that his office could review the documents. But according to Deadline and Variety, he's now dropped his […]
content: Oregon Attorney General Dan Rayfield had been seeking documents from Paramount related to its takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery. Rayfield also asked a state circuit court judge to delay the closing of the deal by 60 days so that his office could review the documents. But according to Deadline and Variety, he’s now dropped his civil investigative demand for the records. Obviously, Paramount is pleased with Rayfield’s decision to withdraw his request, but the AG’s office isn’t exactly satisfied with the outcome. Jenny Hansson, communications director for Rayfield, told Deadline that, “Paramount made it clear that they weren’t going to comply with the investigative demand, and that they think they’re above the law. We’re not going to let them waste Oregonians’ resources on these games. We’ve withdrawn the motion to consider our next steps.” Rayfield specifically was interested in documents relating to Paramount’s lobbying efforts, which were codenamed “Project Warrior.” Paramount is run by David and Larry Ellison, major supporters of Donald Trump who enjoy a cozy relationship with the White House. The President went out of his way during the proceeding to say that Netflix, Paramount’s primary rival for Warner Bros. Discovery, would “pay the consequences” if it didn’t remove Trump critic Susan Rice from its board. While Oregon’s efforts to stop the merger have stalled, others, including California, New York, and even the UK, are considering moves to block the deal on antitrust grounds. Hollywood has also spoken out in opposition to the merger.
chineseTitle: 俄勒冈州总检察长撤回推迟派拉蒙和华纳兄弟合并的努力
chineseSummary: 俄勒冈州总检察长丹·雷菲尔德一直在向派拉蒙寻求有关其收购华纳兄弟探索公司的文件。雷菲尔德还要求州巡回法院法官将交易完成时间推迟 60 天，以便他的办公室可以审查这些文件。但根据 Deadline 和 Variety 的报道，他现在放弃了他的 [...]
chineseContent: 俄勒冈州总检察长丹·雷菲尔德一直在向派拉蒙寻求有关其收购华纳兄弟探索公司的文件。雷菲尔德还要求州巡回法院法官将交易完成时间推迟 60 天，以便他的办公室可以审查这些文件。但据 Deadline 和 Variety 报道，他现在放弃了对这些记录的民事调查要求。显然，派拉蒙对雷菲尔德撤回请求的决定感到满意，但总检察长办公室对结果并不完全满意。雷菲尔德公关总监珍妮·汉森 (Jenny Hansson) 告诉 Deadline，“派拉蒙明确表示他们不会遵守调查要求，他们认为自己凌驾于法律之上。我们不会让他们在这些游戏上浪费俄勒冈人的资源。我们已经撤回了考虑下一步行动的动议。”雷菲尔德对与派拉蒙游说工作相关的文件特别感兴趣，这些文件的代号为“战士计划”。派拉蒙由大卫·埃里森和拉里·埃里森经营，他们是唐纳德·特朗普的主要支持者，与白宫关系融洽。总统在诉讼过程中特意表示，派拉蒙影业华纳兄弟探索频道的主要竞争对手 Netflix 如果不​​将特朗普批评者苏珊·赖斯从董事会中除名，将“承担后果”。虽然俄勒冈州阻止合并的努力已经停滞，但包括加利福尼亚州、纽约州甚至英国在内的其他州正在考虑采取措施以反垄断为由阻止这项交易。好莱坞也公开反对合并。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
